name: Shared Data Models & Enums
description: All Pydantic models, enums, and type definitions shared across Arbiter. Pure data definitions with Pydantic validation — no business logic. Organized as arbiter/models/ package with sub-modules: enums.py, types.py, trust.py, graph.py, findings.py, api.py, canary.py, signals.py, and __init__.py re-exports. All structured data boundaries in the system are defined here. Enforces frozen immutability on ledger/finding models, strict extra='forbid' on API inputs, trust/authority separation via module boundaries, and deterministic serialization for JSONL compatibility.

types.name: TrustTier
types.description: StrEnum for trust classification tiers. No ordering operators — policy must use raw TrustScore, never tier comparisons. Explicit string values for stable serialization.

types.name: DataTier
types.description: StrEnum for data classification tiers. Determines sensitivity level and handling requirements.

types.name: BlastTier
types.description: StrEnum for blast-radius classification tiers. Determines rollout strategy for changes.

types.name: FindingSeverity
types.description: StrEnum for finding severity levels in audit results.

types.name: NodeId
types.description: Annotated[str] constrained type alias for node identifiers. Non-empty string, max 255 chars, must match pattern [a-zA-Z0-9._-]+. Transparent to mypy.

types.name: TrustScore
types.description: Annotated[float] constrained type alias for trust scores. Range [0.0, 1.0] inclusive. Raw IEEE 754 float with no application-level rounding. Policy calculations always use this raw value, never display tiers.

types.name: Sha256Hex
types.description: Annotated[str] constrained type alias for SHA-256 hex digest strings. Exactly 64 lowercase hex characters. Regex: ^[0-9a-f]{64}$.

types.name: UtcDatetime
types.description: Annotated[str] constrained type alias for UTC ISO 8601 timestamps. Must end with 'Z' or '+00:00'. Produced via datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(). Stored as string for JSONL round-trip fidelity.

types.name: AuthorityDomain
types.description: Annotated[str] constrained type alias for authority domain identifiers. Non-empty, max 255 chars, must match pattern [a-zA-Z0-9._/-]+. Authority is declared (from manifests), distinct from trust.

types.name: SequenceNumber
types.description: Annotated[int] constrained type alias for ledger sequence numbers. Non-negative integer (>= 0). Monotonically increasing within a ledger.

types.name: TrustEventType
types.description: StrEnum for trust event categories recorded in the ledger.

types.name: TrustLedgerEntry

fields.name: ts
fields.description: UTC timestamp when the event occurred.

fields.name: node
fields.description: The node this trust event pertains to.

fields.name: event
fields.description: Category of the trust event.

fields.name: weight
fields.description: Signed weight of this event's impact on trust score. Positive increases trust, negative decreases.

validators.error_message: Event weight must be in range [-1.0, 1.0]

fields.name: score_before
fields.description: Trust score of the node immediately before this event was applied.

fields.name: score_after
fields.description: Trust score of the node immediately after this event was applied.

fields.name: sequence_number
fields.description: Monotonically increasing sequence number within the ledger.

fields.name: detail
fields.description: Human-readable detail about the event. Must include the specific node, field, or domain that caused it.

types.description: A single immutable trust event in the append-only ledger. ConfigDict(frozen=True, extra='forbid'). Represents one trust-affecting event for a specific node. The ledger is treated like a financial ledger — no updates, no deletes.

types.name: LedgerCheckpoint

fields.name: ts
fields.description: UTC timestamp when the checkpoint was created.

fields.name: sequence_number
fields.description: Sequence number of the last entry covered by this checkpoint.

fields.name: checksum
fields.description: SHA-256 hex digest of all ledger entries from the previous checkpoint (or start) up to and including sequence_number.

fields.name: entry_count
fields.description: Number of entries covered by this checkpoint.

validators.error_message: Checkpoint must cover at least one entry.

types.description: SHA-256 checksum line inserted into the JSONL ledger after every N entries. ConfigDict(frozen=True, extra='forbid'). Used for integrity verification of the append-only ledger.

types.name: LedgerLine
types.description: Discriminated union of lines that can appear in the JSONL trust ledger. Either a TrustLedgerEntry or a LedgerCheckpoint. Used for typed deserialization of ledger files.

types.name: AccessGraphNode

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique identifier for this node in the access graph.

fields.name: data_access
fields.description: List of DataTier values this node has access to.

fields.name: authority_domains
fields.description: List of AuthorityDomain values declared for this node (from manifests). Authority is declared, not earned.

fields.name: edges
fields.description: List of NodeId references to adjacent nodes in the access graph. String-based for adjacency list representation.

fields.name: trust_tier
fields.description: Current trust tier classification of this node. Display only — policy must use raw TrustScore.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Optional metadata key-value pairs for this node.

types.description: A node in the access graph representing a component/service. ConfigDict(frozen=True, extra='forbid'). Edges are string-based NodeId references (adjacency list) — no circular Pydantic references.

types.name: AccessGraph

fields.name: nodes
fields.description: Mapping from NodeId to AccessGraphNode. All edge targets in any node must exist as keys in this dict (enforced by model validator).

fields.name: version
fields.description: Schema version string for the access graph.

types.description: Container for the full access graph. Mutable (not frozen) to allow incremental construction. Has a model validator ensuring referential integrity: all edge targets must exist as keys in nodes dict. Uses dict[NodeId, AccessGraphNode] for O(1) node lookup.

types.name: ConsistencyFinding

fields.name: ts
fields.description: UTC timestamp when the finding was produced.

fields.name: node
fields.description: The node where inconsistency was detected.

fields.name: severity
fields.description: Severity level of this finding.

fields.name: field
fields.description: The specific field that is inconsistent.

fields.name: adapter_value
fields.description: The ground-truth value from the adapter layer.

fields.name: claimed_value
fields.description: The self-reported value claimed by the node.

fields.name: detail
fields.description: Human-readable explanation including the specific node, field, or domain.

types.description: A finding from consistency analysis between adapter-reported ground truth and node self-reports. ConfigDict(frozen=True, extra='forbid').

types.name: AccessFinding

fields.name: ts
fields.description: UTC timestamp when the finding was produced.

fields.name: node
fields.description: The node with the access issue.

fields.name: severity
fields.description: Severity level of this finding.

fields.name: data_tier
fields.description: The data tier involved in the access issue.

fields.name: authority_domain
fields.description: The authority domain relevant to the finding.

fields.name: violation_type
fields.description: Type of access violation (e.g. 'unauthorized_access', 'excessive_privilege', 'missing_authority').

fields.name: detail
fields.description: Human-readable explanation including the specific node, field, or domain.

types.description: A finding from access audit analysis. ConfigDict(frozen=True, extra='forbid').

types.name: TaintFinding

fields.name: ts
fields.description: UTC timestamp when the finding was produced.

fields.name: source_node
fields.description: The node where tainted data originated.

fields.name: sink_node
fields.description: The node where tainted data arrived.

fields.name: severity
fields.description: Severity level of this finding.

fields.name: data_tier
fields.description: The data classification tier of the tainted data.

fields.name: path
fields.description: Ordered list of NodeIds representing the taint propagation path from source to sink.

fields.name: detail
fields.description: Human-readable explanation including the specific nodes, fields, or domains.

types.description: A finding from taint/data-flow analysis. ConfigDict(frozen=True, extra='forbid').

types.name: ConflictRecord

fields.name: ts
fields.description: UTC timestamp when the conflict was detected.

fields.name: conflict_id
fields.description: Unique identifier for this conflict.

fields.name: nodes
fields.description: List of NodeIds involved in the conflict.

validators.error_message: A conflict must involve at least two nodes.

fields.name: authority_domain
fields.description: The authority domain where the conflict exists.

fields.name: conflict_type
fields.description: Category of conflict (e.g. 'overlapping_authority', 'contradictory_claims').

fields.name: detail
fields.description: Human-readable description including the specific nodes and domain.

fields.name: resolved
fields.description: Whether this conflict has been resolved.

types.description: A record of a detected conflict between nodes or authority claims. ConfigDict(frozen=True, extra='forbid').

types.name: StigmerySignal

fields.name: ts
fields.description: UTC timestamp when the signal was emitted.

fields.name: signal_id
fields.description: Unique identifier for this signal.

fields.name: source_node
fields.description: The node that emitted this signal.

fields.name: signal_type
fields.description: Type/category of the stigmergy signal.

fields.name: payload
fields.description: Arbitrary key-value payload for the signal.

fields.name: ttl_seconds
fields.description: Time-to-live in seconds for this signal. Must be positive.

validators.error_message: TTL must be at least 1 second.

types.description: A stigmergic coordination signal for indirect communication between Arbiter components. ConfigDict(frozen=True, extra='forbid').

types.name: CanaryRecord

fields.name: ts
fields.description: UTC timestamp when the canary was deployed.

fields.name: canary_id
fields.description: Unique identifier for this canary instance.

fields.name: fingerprint
fields.description: Canary fingerprint: structurally valid for its data tier but contains embedded UUID making it recognizable as synthetic and impossible in real data. Must match pattern containing a UUID v4 segment.

validators.error_message: Canary fingerprint must contain a valid UUID v4 segment to be recognizable as synthetic.

fields.name: data_tier
fields.description: The data classification tier this canary is designed for.

fields.name: target_node
fields.description: The node where this canary was injected.

fields.name: triggered
fields.description: Whether this canary has been triggered (detected at an unexpected location).

fields.name: triggered_at
fields.description: UTC timestamp when the canary was triggered, empty if not triggered.

fields.name: triggered_by_node
fields.description: NodeId of the node that triggered the canary, empty if not triggered.

types.description: A record of a canary (synthetic test data) injection and its status. ConfigDict(frozen=True, extra='forbid'). Canary fingerprints must be structurally valid for their tier but globally unique per run and impossible in real data (UUID-in-domain-string pattern).

types.name: FeedbackReportSection

fields.name: section_name
fields.description: Name/title of this report section.

fields.name: content
fields.description: Textual content of this section.

fields.name: findings_count
fields.description: Number of findings summarized in this section.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Optional section metadata.

types.description: A single section within a feedback report. ConfigDict(frozen=True, extra='forbid').

types.name: FeedbackReport

fields.name: ts
fields.description: UTC timestamp when the report was generated.

fields.name: report_id
fields.description: Unique identifier for this report.

fields.name: sections
fields.description: Ordered list of report sections.

fields.name: total_findings
fields.description: Total findings across all sections.

fields.name: generated_by
fields.description: The node/component that generated this report.

types.description: A complete feedback report containing multiple sections. ConfigDict(frozen=True, extra='forbid').

types.name: ErrorResponse

fields.name: error_code
fields.description: Machine-readable error code (e.g. 'INVALID_NODE_ID', 'LEDGER_INTEGRITY_ERROR').

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable error message including the specific node, field, or domain that caused the error.

fields.name: details
fields.description: Optional structured details. Should include 'node', 'field', or 'domain' keys identifying the offending entity.

types.description: Standard HTTP API error response. All API error responses use this structure. Machine-readable error_code plus human-readable message plus optional details including the offending node/field/domain.

types.name: TrustScoreRequest

fields.name: node
fields.description: The node whose trust score is being requested.

types.description: HTTP API request to query a node's trust score. ConfigDict(extra='forbid').

types.name: TrustScoreResponse

fields.name: node
fields.description: The node this score pertains to.

fields.name: score
fields.description: The raw trust score. Policy calculations use this value.

fields.name: tier
fields.description: Display tier classification. For display only — never use for policy decisions.

fields.name: ledger_sequence
fields.description: The ledger sequence number at which this score was computed.

types.description: HTTP API response containing a node's trust score and tier.

types.name: BlastRadiusRequest

fields.name: node
fields.description: The node to compute blast radius for.

fields.name: max_depth
fields.description: Maximum traversal depth for BFS/DFS over access graph edges.

types.description: HTTP API request to compute blast radius for a node. ConfigDict(extra='forbid').

types.name: BlastRadiusResponse

fields.name: node
fields.description: The node analyzed.

fields.name: blast_tier
fields.description: Computed blast tier classification determining rollout strategy.

fields.name: affected_nodes
fields.description: List of NodeIds reachable within the blast radius.

fields.name: affected_data_tiers
fields.description: Data classification tiers reachable in the blast radius.

fields.name: depth_reached
fields.description: Actual traversal depth reached.

types.description: HTTP API response containing blast radius analysis results.

types.name: FindingsRequest

fields.name: node
fields.description: Optional node filter. If provided, only findings for this node are returned.

fields.name: severity_min
fields.description: Minimum severity to include.

fields.name: limit
fields.description: Maximum number of findings to return.

types.description: HTTP API request to query findings. ConfigDict(extra='forbid').

types.name: FindingsResponse

fields.name: consistency_findings
fields.description: Consistency findings matching the query.

fields.name: access_findings
fields.description: Access findings matching the query.

fields.name: taint_findings
fields.description: Taint findings matching the query.

fields.name: total_count
fields.description: Total number of findings matching the query (may exceed returned count if limit applied).

types.description: HTTP API response containing queried findings.

types.name: HealthResponse

fields.name: status
fields.description: Health status: 'healthy' or 'degraded'.

fields.name: version
fields.description: Arbiter version string.

fields.name: ledger_sequence
fields.description: Current ledger sequence number.

fields.name: uptime_seconds
fields.description: Uptime in seconds since startup.

types.description: HTTP API response for health check endpoint.

types.name: Claim

fields.name: source_node
fields.description: The node making this claim.

fields.name: claimed_at
fields.description: UTC timestamp when the claim was made.

fields.name: claim_type
fields.description: Type/category of the claim for dispatch.

fields.name: payload
fields.description: The claimed data as a dict. Structure depends on claim_type.

fields.name: verified
fields.description: Whether this claim has been verified against adapter ground truth.

fields.name: verification_ts
fields.description: UTC timestamp of verification, empty if unverified.

types.description: Generic wrapper for self-reported data from a node. Marks data as a claim (not ground truth). Trust is earned (computed from ledger), but node self-reports are claims that must be verified against the adapter layer.

types.name: ClassificationRule

fields.name: field_pattern
fields.description: fnmatch or regex pattern to match field names against.

fields.name: data_tier
fields.description: The data tier to assign when the pattern matches.

fields.name: is_regex
fields.description: If true, field_pattern is a regex; if false, it is an fnmatch pattern.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Human-readable description of what this rule classifies.

types.description: A field classification rule from the classification registry. Uses fnmatch or regex patterns. ConfigDict(frozen=True, extra='forbid').

types.name: ValidationErrorDetail

fields.name: field
fields.description: The field that failed validation.

fields.name: value
fields.description: String representation of the invalid value.

fields.name: constraint
fields.description: The constraint that was violated.

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable error message.

types.description: Detailed information about a single validation error for structured error reporting.

functions.name: create_trust_ledger_entry
functions.description: Factory function to create a validated TrustLedgerEntry with automatic timestamp. Ensures all invariants are met: score_after must equal score_before + weight clamped to [0.0, 1.0], timestamp is UTC, and detail is non-empty for AUDIT_FAIL and ACCESS_VIOLATION events.

inputs.name: node
inputs.description: The node this trust event pertains to.

inputs.name: weight
inputs.description: Signed weight of this event's impact.

inputs.name: detail
inputs.description: Human-readable detail. Must include the specific node, field, or domain.

error_cases.name: invalid_node_id
error_cases.condition: node does not match NodeId pattern [a-zA-Z0-9._-]+

error_cases.name: missing_detail_for_critical_event
error_cases.condition: event is AUDIT_FAIL or ACCESS_VIOLATION and detail is empty

preconditions: score_before is a valid TrustScore in [0.0, 1.0]
For AUDIT_FAIL and ACCESS_VIOLATION events, detail must be non-empty
postconditions: Returned entry.score_after == clamp(score_before + weight, 0.0, 1.0)
Returned entry.ts is a valid UTC ISO 8601 timestamp
Returned entry is frozen (immutable)
Returned entry.sequence_number == input sequence_number

functions.name: create_ledger_checkpoint
functions.description: Factory function to create a validated LedgerCheckpoint. Computes no checksum itself — caller must provide the pre-computed SHA-256 checksum. Validates all fields and returns a frozen instance.

inputs.name: sequence_number
inputs.description: Sequence number of the last entry covered.

inputs.name: checksum
inputs.description: SHA-256 hex digest of covered entries.

validators.error_message: Must cover at least one entry.

preconditions: checksum is a valid 64-character lowercase hex string
postconditions: Returned checkpoint is frozen (immutable)
Returned checkpoint.ts is a valid UTC ISO 8601 timestamp

functions.name: build_access_graph
functions.description: Constructs a validated AccessGraph from a dict of AccessGraphNode instances. Performs referential integrity validation: all edge targets in every node must exist as keys in the nodes dict. Returns an AccessGraph or raises on integrity violation.

inputs.name: nodes
inputs.description: Mapping from NodeId string to AccessGraphNode instance.

error_cases.name: dangling_edge
error_cases.condition: A node's edge list contains a NodeId that does not exist in the nodes dict

error_cases.name: id_key_mismatch
error_cases.condition: A node's id field does not match its key in the dict

error_cases.name: invalid_node_id
error_cases.condition: A key in the dict is not a valid NodeId

preconditions: All keys in nodes are valid NodeIds
Each node's id field matches its key in the dict
All edge targets in every node reference keys that exist in nodes
postconditions: Returned AccessGraph passes referential integrity: for all nodes, all edges point to existing nodes
Returned AccessGraph.nodes has same cardinality as input
Each node's id matches its dict key

functions.name: parse_ledger_line
functions.description: Parses a single JSONL line from the trust ledger into the appropriate LedgerLine variant (TrustLedgerEntry or LedgerCheckpoint). Uses discriminated union deserialization. The line must be valid JSON and conform to one of the two schemas.

inputs.name: line
inputs.description: A single JSON line from the JSONL ledger file.

error_cases.name: unknown_schema
error_cases.condition: JSON does not match TrustLedgerEntry or LedgerCheckpoint schema

error_cases.name: validation_failure
error_cases.condition: JSON matches a schema but field values fail validation

postconditions: Returned value is either a TrustLedgerEntry or LedgerCheckpoint
All fields pass Pydantic validation
Float trust scores round-trip exactly (IEEE 754 fidelity)

functions.name: serialize_ledger_line
functions.description: Serializes a LedgerLine (TrustLedgerEntry or LedgerCheckpoint) to a JSON string suitable for appending to a JSONL file. Ensures deterministic serialization order and IEEE 754 float fidelity for trust scores.

error_cases.name: invalid_entry_type
error_cases.condition: entry is not a TrustLedgerEntry or LedgerCheckpoint

preconditions: entry is a valid TrustLedgerEntry or LedgerCheckpoint
postconditions: Output is a single-line valid JSON string (no embedded newlines)
parse_ledger_line(serialize_ledger_line(entry)) produces a value equal to entry
Float values have full IEEE 754 round-trip fidelity
JSON keys are in deterministic (sorted) order

functions.name: create_error_response
functions.description: Factory function to create a standardized ErrorResponse. Ensures error messages include the specific node, field, or domain that caused the error as required by project standards.

inputs.name: message
inputs.description: Human-readable message. Must reference the offending entity.

preconditions: At least one of node, field, or domain should be provided for specificity
postconditions: Returned ErrorResponse.error_code == input error_code
Returned ErrorResponse.details contains non-empty 'node', 'field', and/or 'domain' keys for any provided values
Returned ErrorResponse.message is non-empty

functions.name: validate_canary_fingerprint
functions.description: Validates that a canary fingerprint contains an embedded UUID v4, making it recognizable as synthetic data that is impossible in real data. Returns True if valid, raises ValueError otherwise.

inputs.name: fingerprint
inputs.description: The canary fingerprint string to validate.

error_cases.name: missing_uuid
error_cases.condition: fingerprint does not contain a UUID v4 segment

postconditions: Returns True only if fingerprint contains a valid UUID v4 segment matching [0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-4[0-9a-f]{3}-[89ab][0-9a-f]{3}-[0-9a-f]{12}

functions.name: score_to_tier
functions.description: Converts a raw TrustScore to its display TrustTier. This is for display/reporting ONLY — all policy calculations must use the raw score, never the tier. Tier boundaries: [0.0, 0.2) -> PROBATIONARY, [0.2, 0.4) -> LOW, [0.4, 0.6) -> ESTABLISHED, [0.6, 0.8) -> HIGH, [0.8, 1.0] -> TRUSTED.
postconditions: Returned tier corresponds to the correct bracket for the input score
This function is deterministic: same input always yields same output

functions.name: classify_field
functions.description: Classifies a field name against a list of ClassificationRule instances, returning the matching DataTier. Uses fnmatch or regex depending on the rule's is_regex flag. Returns the first matching rule's data_tier. If no rule matches, returns DataTier.PUBLIC as the default.

inputs.name: rules
inputs.description: Ordered list of ClassificationRule instances to match against.

error_cases.name: invalid_regex_pattern
error_cases.condition: A rule with is_regex=True has an invalid regex pattern

preconditions: rules is a list (may be empty)
postconditions: If any rule matches, returns the data_tier of the first matching rule
If no rule matches, returns DataTier.PUBLIC
Rules are evaluated in order; first match wins

invariants: All models with frozen=True config are immutable after construction — no attribute reassignment allowed
All models with extra='forbid' reject unexpected fields during construction
Trust is earned (computed from ledger via TrustScore), authority is declared (from manifests via AuthorityDomain) — these are distinct type aliases and never conflated
The trust ledger is append-only: TrustLedgerEntry and LedgerCheckpoint are frozen, no update or delete operations exist
All policy calculations use raw TrustScore (float), never TrustTier — tier is for display only
TrustScore values are raw IEEE 754 floats in [0.0, 1.0] with no application-level rounding
All timestamps are UTC ISO 8601 strings ending with 'Z' or '+00:00' — no local times
AccessGraph referential integrity: every edge target in every node must exist as a key in AccessGraph.nodes
Canary fingerprints must contain a UUID v4 segment making them recognizable as synthetic and impossible in real data
Error messages and ErrorResponse instances must include the specific node, field, or domain that caused the error
LedgerLine is a discriminated union: every JSONL line is exactly one of TrustLedgerEntry or LedgerCheckpoint
Sequence numbers are monotonically increasing within a ledger — never reused or decremented
Node self-reports are claims (Claim type), the adapter layer is ground truth — these are structurally distinct
ClassificationRule evaluation is order-dependent: first matching rule wins
All enum types use StrEnum with explicit string values for stable serialization across versions
